prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial opens on an almost entirely black frame. A “Classic Commercial Archives” circular logo appears at the lower left, and a semi-transparent “@ClassicCommercialArchives” watermark runs near the bottom.

By around 23 seconds, the image has changed to a soft, slightly blurred shot involving a car. Only part of the vehicle is visible: the right side/window area and side mirror appear along the right edge of the frame. The background is pale and indistinct, with a diagonal dark line and a bright diamond-shaped area near the center, making the setting difficult to identify from this sampled frame.

The final frame presents a clear end-card style composition. A car is shown driving away on a road through an open landscape, framed inside a large stylized hexagonal or tunnel-like graphic. Above the road scene, bold text reads “THE NEW MONTE CARLO,” and below it is a blue Chevrolet bowtie logo with “Chevrolet” written inside. The same archive watermarking remains visible at the bottom.
